lessons.name: The mental model: Inputs → Logic → Outputs
How to ask clarifying questions (with examples)
The 5 traps that get candidates rejected
Drill: walk through the 911 prompt yourself
Four live phrases that signal senior judgment

existing_cards.q: What is the #1 instant-reject signal in a problem-decomposition / case interview?
existing_cards.a: Jumping to specific tools or solutions before asking clarifying questions and scoping the problem (users, success metric, constraints, data).
1. Clarify (success metric, users, data, constraints). 2. Stakeholder map. 3. Data sources. 4. Tradeoffs. 5. Failure modes.

existing_cards.q: How long should clarifying questions take in a 60-min decomp round?
existing_cards.a: 10–15 minutes. Most candidates rush in under 3 minutes. The interviewer is testing whether you can stay in the problem space before solving — senior candidates linger longer than feels comfortable. (See also fc-dec-6.)

existing_cards.q: I → L → O mental model — what goes where?
existing_cards.a: Inputs = data sources (logs, APIs, files). Logic = transformations, decisions, joins. Outputs = what the user sees/acts on (dashboards, alerts, reports).

(1) "Before I propose anything…" (2) "The simplest version that delivers value…" (3) "X over Y because of this specific constraint — if it changes I'd flip." (4) "Let me make an explicit assumption: X; please push back."

existing_cards.q: Senior signal: how long should clarifying-question time feel uncomfortable?
existing_cards.a: Past the point where you want to start drawing — typically 10–15 min in a 60-min round. The discomfort is the signal you're doing it right; juniors leave step 1 the moment they have a plausible solution.
Make explicit assumption out loud + invite correction: "I'll assume X — please push back if wrong." Write it on the board.

existing_cards.q: Structure of a good clarifying question?
existing_cards.a: "Are we doing A or B? Because if A, I'd do X; if B, I'd do Y." Specific, falsifiable, branches into design-relevant alternatives.

existing_cards.q: How many failure modes should you name in a decomp answer?
existing_cards.a: 3 specific ones with detection mechanism + rollback. "Add monitoring" alone is junior.

existing_cards.q: The "decomposition shape" — how many solution options?
existing_cards.a: TWO phased options with explicit tradeoffs + recommendation + rationale. One = no tradeoff awareness. Three = indecisive.

existing_cards.q: A senior signal phrase in decomp?
existing_cards.a: "X over Y because of this specific constraint — if it changes I'd flip the decision." Explicit tradeoff + conditional commitment.

existing_cards.q: Hospital adoption (12% after 90 days) — first analytical split?
existing_cards.a: Product gaps vs adoption gaps. Different fixes: product needs product changes; adoption needs training/change-mgmt/incentives.

existing_cards.q: What to draw FIRST on the whiteboard?
existing_cards.a: Inputs → Logic → Outputs. Boxes describe BEHAVIOR (not tech) until you discuss implementation choices.
